title: langloisia setosissima subsp. setosissima
intro: Langloisia setosissima subsp. setosissima, also known as the Pointless Keckella, is a rare, succulent plant native to California. This plant is used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The Pointless Keckella is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, salves, and tinctures, which are used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to support overall well-being.
